name: Mexican Stuffed Mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. Twist mushroom stems free and trim off dried ends. Finely chop the stems.
2. In a 10-12 inch frying pan over medium heat, combine the chopped stems, green onions, garlic, cumin, chili powder, and 1/4 cup water. Stir often until the vegetables begin to brown.
3. Add 2 tablespoons water, scrape any browned bits from the bottom of the pan, and repeat step 2.
4. Add tomato sauce, chilies, and half the cheese. Remove from heat and gently stir in the stuffing mix.
5. Rub mushrooms with salad oil. Set mushroom caps side up in a 9x13 inch pan. Mound all the filling into the caps. Sprinkle with remaining cheese.
6. Bake at 400°F until the cheese is lightly browned, about 15-20 minutes.
Per serving: 199 calories; 9.6 grams protein; 8.7 grams fat; (3.6 grams saturated fat); 23 grams carbohydrates; 731 milligrams sodium; 20 milligrams cholesterol.
